generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis via current input (I_M) and producing torque (τ_M) for the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot."
]

instance GearTransmissionSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gear transmission system comprising gearwheel and cycloidal gearbox that reduces speed and increases torque output (τ_G and τ_out) for the first axis."
]

instance SiemensSinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller enabling separate axis control with configurable inputs/outputs for position, speed, and acceleration."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor current (I_M) for torque estimation via manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Dual-position encoder measuring motor angular position and velocity (q_M, ω_M) for real-time feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder behind the gearbox measuring output angular position and velocity (q_out, ω_out) for backlash and elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring system temperature for friction model parameter adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the first axis components (Motor, Gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance SysMLBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) describing rotational/current flows and system properties at creation time."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) providing contextual metadata including system properties and configuration data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTIntegrationEnabler
]

instance TorqueVelocityDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model deriving torque (τ_M) and velocity (ω_M) from motor current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink model simulating ideal gearbox behavior without friction for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for dynamic exchange."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink friction model implementing Stribeck friction characteristics for dynamic exchange."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink friction model implementing Dahl friction characteristics for dynamic exchange."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink friction model implementing LuGre friction characteristics for dynamic exchange."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured motor current (I_M) data trace from physical sensor."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueVelocityDerivationModel
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor torque (τ_M) calculated from motor current (I_M) using manufacturer dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance DerivedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor velocity (ω_M) calculated from motor current (I_M) and torque (τ_M) using manufacturer dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured output torque (τ_out) from gearbox position encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance MeasuredOutputVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Measured output velocity (ω_out) from gearbox position encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MatlabSimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulatedMotorVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated motor velocity (ω̇_M) output from friction-corrected simulation model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealGearboxBehaviorModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationProcessingComponent
]

instance SimulatedGearboxVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearbox velocity (ω̇_G) output from friction-corrected simulation model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealGearboxBehaviorModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationProcessingComponent
]

instance SimulatedOutputVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated output velocity (ω̇_out) output from friction-corrected simulation model."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IdealGearboxBehaviorModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationProcessingComponent
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MatlabSimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment executing ideal behavior and friction models for dynamic exchange."
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorSimulationService, GearboxSimulationService, FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance MQTTIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time sensor data streaming from Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AAS implementation for metadata management and contextual data linking."
    DTDFVocab:enables MetadataManagementService
]

instance SimulationProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component consuming input data traces (e.g., I_M) and producing simulated outputs (e.g., ω̇_M)."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionCorrectionService
]

// C6: Services
instance MotorSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation service modeling ideal motor behavior without friction for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotorBehaviorInsight
]

instance GearboxSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation service modeling ideal gearbox behavior without friction for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:provides GearboxBehaviorInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing friction-induced deviations using combined white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionParameterInsight, ControlOptimizationInsight
]

instance FrictionCorrectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service applying friction correction to simulated outputs using dynamic friction models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCorrectedOutputsInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service streaming real-time sensor data via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceAvailabilityInsight
]

instance MetadataManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing asset metadata and contextual data linking."
    DTDFVocab:provides ContextualDataInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance MotorBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into ideal motor behavior providing baseline for friction analysis."
]

instance GearboxBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into ideal gearbox behavior providing baseline for friction analysis."
]

instance FrictionParameterInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction coefficients for model refinement."
]

instance ControlOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insights into deviation sources (e.g., gearbox wear, temperature effects) to improve motion accuracy."
]

instance FrictionCorrectedOutputsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics comparing simulated friction-corrected outputs with reference measurements."
]

instance DataTraceAvailabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of continuous data trace availability from physical sensors."
]

instance ContextualDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Access to system properties and configuration metadata for contextual analysis."
]

instance FrictionParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic update of friction model parameters based on real-time measurements."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of machining parameters (speed, load) based on friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation of alerts for early detection of friction anomalies indicating potential wear."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearTransmissionSystem, SiemensSinumerik840DslController,
                 MotorCurrentSensor, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder, TemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SysMLStructuralModel, SysMLBehavioralModel, AssetAdministrationShell,
                 TorqueVelocityDerivationModel, IdealGearboxBehaviorModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel,
                 StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel,
                 MatlabSimulinkEnabler, MQTTIntegrationEnabler, AssetAdministrationShellEnabler,
                 SimulationProcessingComponent
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale**, synchronized with the physical system’s operational frequency. Key time-scale specifics include:
  - **Sensor data acquisition rate**: Not explicitly stated, but implied to be **high-frequency** (e.g., ≥1 kHz for encoder data) to capture dynamic friction effects.
  - **Model update frequency**: Dynamic exchange of friction models occurs **during operation** to adapt to wear, temperature, or load changes.
  - **Dashboard refresh rate**: Visualization updates in **real-time** to reflect live data and processed friction analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is orchestrated as follows:
  - **Centralized processing**: All simulation models (*Simulation M*, *Simulation GW*, *Simulation GB*) and data traces are managed within a unified **digital shadow** framework (FrictionShadow).
  - **Data flow**:
    1. Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) → **MQTT** → Digital Shadow (data traces).
    2. Data traces → **Processing components** (simulation models) → Simulated outputs.
    3. Simulated outputs → **Comparison with reference data** → Friction analysis.
  - **Component interactions**:
    - **Sources** (sensors/calculations) feed data traces into **Processing components** (simulation models).
    - **Models** (engineering, simulation) are linked to **assets** (Mabi robot axis) via SysML descriptions.
    - **Metadata** (e.g., system properties) is stored separately for traceability.
  - **Visualization**: Dashboard aggregates all data traces, models, and processing results for operational insights."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical system’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation:
  - **Design phase**: Not explicitly addressed; the DT is built post-deployment for **continuous improvement** of motion accuracy.
  - **Manufacturing phase**: Not applicable; the DT targets **runtime friction analysis** during machining operations.
  - **Operational phase**: Primary focus on **real-time friction modeling and control optimization** via live data integration. The DT enables **adaptive model updates** to account for wear, tool changes, or environmental factors (e.g., temperature)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**:
    - **Comparison of simulated outputs (ˆ*r_M*, ˆ*r_G*, ˆ*r_out*)** with **reference measurements** (*r_M*, *r_G*, *r_out*) to quantify friction-induced deviations.
    - **Uncertainty analysis** via backlash reduction using dual encoders and manufacturer-provided motor constants.
  - **Verification Mechanisms**:
    - **Traceability** of data traces to their **sources** (sensors/calculations) and **processing components** (simulation models).
    - **Metadata** (e.g., system properties, time stamps) for contextual validation.
  - **Error Handling**:
    - **Dynamic model exchange** mitigates parameter drift due to wear or environmental changes.
    - **Friction models** (white-box/black-box) are validated against **operational data** to ensure accuracy over time.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**:
    - **Sensor noise** in encoder/current measurements.
    - **Model simplifications** (e.g., ideal gearbox behavior assumptions).
    - **Parameter variability** (e.g., temperature-dependent friction coefficients)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **real-time sensor data streaming** (e.g., *I_M*, *q_out*) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Data Format**: **Time-series data traces** (arrays of torque/velocity values) with **metadata** (source, time stamps).
  - **Synchronization**: **Continuous, one-way data flow** from PT to DT (digital shadow) with **no feedback control** (as per digital shadow definition).
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low-latency due to MQTT’s real-time capabilities and operational requirements."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital twin is **hosted in a centralized computational environment** supporting:
  - **Simulation Software**: **MATLAB/Simulink** for executable friction models.
  - **Data Processing**: **Processing components** (e.g., *Simulation M*) running on a **local or cloud-based server** (not specified).
  - **Visualization**: **Dashboard** integrated with the digital shadow framework for operational monitoring.
  - **Deployment Architecture**:
    - **Physical Layer**: Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller with embedded sensors.
    - **Virtual Layer**: Digital shadow framework (DSRM concretization) with **SysML models**, **Simulink simulations**, and **MQTT integration**.
    - **No remote actuation**: The DT is **passive** (digital shadow) and does not send commands back to the PT."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML** (OMG standard) for **system modeling** (Block Definition Diagrams, Internal Block Diagrams).
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (Industry 4.0 standard) for **metadata management** and **contextual data linking**.
  - **MQTT** (OASIS standard) for **real-time data streaming** from the CNC controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) for **multi-physics modeling**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin incorporates the following security and safety considerations:
  - **Passive Operation**: As a **digital shadow**, the DT does not execute **remote commands** on the physical system, eliminating direct safety risks (e.g., human accidents).
  - **Data Integrity**: **MQTT-based streaming** ensures **encrypted data transmission** (implied by industrial standards) to prevent tampering.
  - **Access Control**: **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** metadata includes **system properties** for configuration validation, reducing unauthorized access risks.
  - **Safety Constraints**:
    - **Operational limits** (e.g., axis range [-90°, 90°], max speed 75°/s) are enforced by the **CNC controller**, not the DT.
    - **No feedback loop**: The DT does not alter physical behavior, mitigating safety hazards from virtual-to-physical interactions.
  - **Cybersecurity**: While not explicitly detailed, **industrial-grade protocols** (MQTT, AAS) are assumed to follow **IEC 62443** standards for cybersecurity in manufacturing."
]
